title: rubus hirtus var. kosaninii
intro: Rubus hirtus var. kosaninii, also known as the Balkan raspberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the Balkan Peninsula, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. kosaninii include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for topical and internal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
